political.s: <cite index="2-15">Truist sponsors political action committees that allow teammates, corporate directors, and PAC advisory board members to voluntarily pool financial resources to support candidates meaningful to business operations.</cite> <cite index="3-3,3-4">The company fails to disclose its payments to trade associations and social welfare groups, or amounts used for lobbying, and belongs to organizations like the ABA, BPI, and US Chamber of Commerce that together spent $119.9 million on federal lobbying in 2022.</cite>

charity.s: <cite index="19-1,19-3,19-4">Over $99 million in total giving was deployed in 2023 through the Truist Foundation, Charitable Fund and CRA philanthropic grants, including hundreds of grants supporting economic mobility, small businesses, and low- to moderate-income communities.</cite> <cite index="17-6,17-8">Since 2021, Truist invested in organizations and initiatives helping create pathways for over 25,000 workers and assisted over 18,000 entrepreneurs.</cite>
charity.focus: Economic mobility, small business development, workforce training, affordable housing, community development

environment.s: <cite index="7-1,7-10">Truist set a goal of net-zero greenhouse gas emissions by 2050, and in 2021 supported $15.3 billion of investment grade ESG-themed bond issuance and facilitated renewable energy development with direct capital commitments exceeding $856 million.</cite> <cite index="3-3,3-5">However, Truist fails to disclose its payments to trade associations and social welfare groups used for lobbying, presenting reputational risks when lobbying contradicts company public positions.</cite>

labor.s: <cite index="26-1,26-2">Truist has a DEI rating of 3.7 out of 5 stars based on 2,432 employee ratings, equal to the average for DEI ratings in the Financial Services sector.</cite> <cite index="30-10,30-11">In 2021, women represented 63.5% of workforce with 28.6% at senior management level.</cite> <cite index="22-1,22-4,22-5">Recently, Truist shifted language in filings from emphasizing DEI to promoting 'belonging and inclusivity' in response to political pressures.</cite>

dei.s: <cite index="21-7">Truist has 43% diverse board of directors and 21% diverse executive leadership, with eight Business Resource Groups and required DEI training for all teammates.</cite> <cite index="22-3,22-5">However, Truist recently de-emphasized DEI terminology in regulatory filings, shifting to 'belonging and inclusivity' language amid Trump administration political pressures.</cite>

animals.s: Not applicable for financial services institution.

guns.s: Not applicable as Truist is a financial services provider, not a manufacturer or direct seller of firearms.

privacy.s: <cite index="45-28,45-29">Truist experienced two data breaches in the past two years: a 2023 breach affecting approximately 65,000 people and a 2024 third-party incident affecting several million people.</cite> <cite index="41-3,41-4">Truist adheres to an exemption within the CCPA for data collected pursuant to the Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act, though it provides comprehensive privacy notices and allows data subject rights requests.</cite>

execPay.s: <cite index="37-2,37-5">CEO Bill Rogers received $13.95 million in 2024 total compensation, comprised of 8.6% salary and 91.4% bonuses including stock awards.</cite> <cite index="37-9">His compensation of $13.95 million is about average for companies of similar size in the US market.</cite> Specific CEO-to-worker pay ratio not disclosed in available sources.

fec.committeeName: TRUIST FINANCIAL CORPORATION FEDERAL, STATE & LOCAL POLITICAL ACTION COMMITTEE (FORMERLY SUNTRUST BANKS, INC. MAIN STREET BANKERS PAC)

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
